title: Comment channel: POST /api/comment + comments.jsonl + kb.py comments CLI
description:
Server-side channel (the contract + the 'emit').

FIRST STEP in worktree: git merge epic/repo-wiki-web is NOT relevant (that branch is gone); merge main: 'git merge main --no-edit'? No — just branch from main which has everything. Verify kb.py has cmd_serve + WikiHandler.

Add POST /api/comment to WikiHandler: parse JSON body {page, section, selected_text, comment}; validate (non-empty comment+selected_text; cap sizes e.g. comment<=4000, selected_text<=2000; sandbox 'page' to the wiki like /api/page); append a record {id (short uuid/time-based), page, section, selected_text, comment, ts (ISO), status:'open'} to <wiki>/.comments/comments.jsonl (create dir). Return {ok:true, id}. This is the ONLY write endpoint; everything else stays read-only.

Add kb.py 'comments' subcommand: 'comments' or 'comments list' (open ones, human-readable), 'comments list --json [--since <id/ts>]' (for the agent watch-loop), 'comments resolve <id> [--note]' (set status:resolved), 'comments clear' (archive/clear). Reuse existing helpers; stdlib only.

Gitignore repo-wiki/.comments/ (init wiring is T4; here just ensure the dir is created and add a .gitignore entry locally for the test).

Test:
- python3 -m py_compile kb.py
- boot serve --wiki <tmp>; curl -X POST /api/comment with JSON -> {ok,id}; file has the record; kb.py comments list shows it; resolve marks it; traversal on page field -> 400.
notes: 2026-06-14 15:32 - [human] ANCHOR: the comment record must include a precise location: 'page' (the path) AND 'line' (1-based start line in the page's raw markdown), plus optional 'end_line'. Final record: {id, page, line, end_line, section, selected_text, comment, ts, status}. The server just stores what the client sends; T2 computes line.
acceptance_criteria: POST /api/comment appends a validated record to .comments/comments.jsonl and returns id; page field sandboxed; kb.py comments list/resolve work; py_compile passes
closed_reason: Completed: POST /api/comment (validated, page sandboxed, 400 on traversal/empty) → .comments/comments.jsonl {id,page,line,end_line,section,selected_text,comment,ts,status}; kb.py comments list/--json --since/resolve/clear; .comments gitignored.
